---
title: "Evaluating OERs"
format: html

previous:
  href: "User-Discovering OERs.qmd"
  text: "Discovering OERs"

next:
  href: "User-Citing and Attribution.qmd"
  text: "Citing and Attribution"
---

### The basics

Treat this process in the same way for evaluating any teaching and learning resource. When selecting an OER, consider:

-   Quality

-   Accuracy

-   Clarity

-   Are there any biases?

### Licensing

-   Does the licence permit your intended use?

#### CC licence suffixes and your rights

{{< meta glossary.CC >}} licences have suffixes which perform different functions. All Creative Commons licnences start CC followed by:

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Attribution (BY)

All Creative Commons licences require "BY" or "0" after CC and before any other suffixes. "BY" makes attribution to the creator a prerequisite of any permissible reuse, whereas "0" waives that requirement.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Non-Commercial (NC)

Reuse for purposes primarily intended for commercial advantage or monetary compensation is not permitted.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## No-Derivatives (ND)

The material may not be shared if it has been adapted, modified, or transformed in any way.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Share Alike (SA)

Requires derivative or remixed outputs to use same licence.
:::

[![](images/clipboard-2765196364.png){fig-alt="Go to next page: User-Citing%20and%20Attribution.html" fig-align="center" width="50"}](/OERs%20for%20users/User-Citing%20and%20Attribution.html)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: nav-footer
```{=html}
<a href="{{< meta previous.href >}}" aria-label="Go to previous page: {{< meta previous.text >}}">
  <span aria-hidden="true">«</span> {{< meta previous.text >}}
</a>

<a href="{{< meta next.href >}}" aria-label="Go to next page: {{< meta next.text >}}">
  {{< meta next.text >}} <span aria-hidden="true">»</span>
</a>
```
:::
